title: Passive Voice & Formal Writing — Reading a Formal Report
objectives: Hiểu và áp dụng: reading a formal report
Học 24 từ vựng theo chủ đề tuần 9
Hoàn thành 40 câu hỏi trắc nghiệm tăng dần độ khó
Bám sát mục tiêu tuần: Passive Voice & Formal Writing
lesson: **Passive Voice & Formal Writing.** We use the passive voice when the action is more important than the person doing it, which is common in **formal** writing. Form the passive with *be* + past participle: 'The letter *was written* by Tom.' Change the tense by changing *be*: present 'is **produced**', past 'was **built**', future 'will be **announced**', present continuous 'is being **repaired**'. The object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive one. Use *by* to mention the agent if needed. The passive is useful for **reports**, processes and scientific writing: 'Penicillin *was discovered* by Fleming'; 'Cars *are produced* in this factory.' Practise turning active sentences into passive ones at different tenses.
